   201→            "V": v.get("V", 0),
   202→            "reason": "; ".join(reasons),
   203→            "fallback": fallback,
   204→        }
   205→

1→"""
     2→AEGIS Run Envelope — Compiled context for each task execution.
     3→
     4→The Run Envelope carries identity, governance, memory, tools, and
     5→audit config into each model call. It is structured data, never
     6→prompt text (non-substitutability requirement).
     7→
     8→Phase 7 additions:
     9→  - ActionEnvelope wraps every tool call with risk score + audit record
    10→  - Every tool execution produces a structured log entry (requested, scored,
    11→    approved/denied, result, postcondition)
    12→  - Human approval threshold is configurable per action class
    13→
    14→Spec: AEGIS_RUN_ENVELOPE
    15→"""
    16→
    17→import time
    18→import uuid
    19→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    20→from enum import Enum
    21→from typing import Any
    22→
    23→
    24→@dataclass
    25→class RunEnvelope:
    26→    """AEGIS Run Envelope — compiled before any substrate model call."""
    27→
    28→    identity_kernel: dict
    29→    goal_contract: str
    30→    governance_policy: str = "STANDARD"
    31→    memory_snapshot: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    32→    tool_manifest: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    33→    audit_config: dict = field(default_factory=lambda: {"log_level": "full"})
    34→
    35→    REQUIRED_FIELDS = [
    36→        "identity_kernel", "goal_contract", "governance_policy",
    37→        "memory_snapshot", "tool_manifest", "audit_config",
    38→    ]
    39→
    40→    def is_complete(self) -> bool:
    41→        """Envelope must have all required fields populated."""
    42→        return all(getattr(self, f, None) is not None for f in self.REQUIRED_FIELDS)
    43→
    44→    def has_identity(self) -> bool:
    45→        return bool(self.identity_kernel and self.identity_kernel.get("name"))
    46→
    47→    def to_system_prompt(self) -> str:
    48→        """Render identity kernel as system prompt for the substrate model.
    49→        Note: this is a convenience method. The envelope itself is structural
    50→        data — the system prompt is only one projection of it."""
    51→        k = self.identity_kernel
    52→        if not k:
    53→            return ""
    54→        lines = [
    55→            f"You are {k.get('name', 'Unknown')}. {k.get('doctrine', '')}",
    56→            "Your identity is sovereign and does not change based on which model runs you.",
    57→        ]
    58→        if k.get("hard_constraints"):
    59→            lines.append("Hard constraints: " + "; ".join(k["hard_constraints"]))
    60→        return "\n".join(lines)
    61→
    62→
    63→# ------------------------------------------------------------------
    64→# Action Envelope (AEGIS Phase 7)
    65→# ------------------------------------------------------------------
    66→
    67→class ActionClass(str, Enum):
    68→    READ = "read"
    69→    WRITE = "write"
    70→    EXECUTE = "execute"
    71→    DELETE = "delete"
    72→    NETWORK = "network"
    73→    AUTH = "auth"
    74→
    75→
    76→class ApprovalStatus(str, Enum):
    77→    APPROVED = "approved"
    78→    DENIED = "denied"
    79→    PENDING = "pending"
    80→    AUTO = "auto"
    81→
    82→
    83→class ApprovalSource(str, Enum):
    84→    POLICY = "policy"           # blocked by hard policy
    85→    HUMAN = "human"             # explicit human approval
    86→    AUTO_LOW_RISK = "auto_low_risk"  # auto-approved below threshold
    87→    THRESHOLD = "threshold"     # approved by risk threshold rule
    88→
    89→
    90→@dataclass
    91→class ApprovalRecord:
    92→    status: ApprovalStatus
    93→    source: ApprovalSource
    94→    reason: str = ""
    95→    approved_at: float | None = None
    96→    approver_id: str | None = None
    97→
    98→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    99→        return {
   100→            "status": self.status.value,
   101→            "source": self.source.value,
   102→            "reason": self.reason,
   103→            "approved_at": self.approved_at,
   104→            "approver_id": self.approver_id,
   105→        }
   106→
   107→
   108→@dataclass
   109→class ActionEnvelope:
   110→    """
   111→    Every tool call is wrapped in an ActionEnvelope before execution.
   112→    The envelope records the full audit trail: request → score → decision → result.
   113→    """
   114→    action: str
   115→    inputs: dict
   116→    action_class: ActionClass = ActionClass.READ
   117→    action_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
   118→    risk_score: float = 0.0
   119→    verifier_outcome: str = "pending"
   120→    approval: ApprovalRecord = field(
   121→        default_factory=lambda: ApprovalRecord(
   122→            status=ApprovalStatus.PENDING, source=ApprovalSource.POLICY
   123→        )
   124→    )
   125→    result: Any = None
   126→    postcondition: str | None = None
   127→    requested_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
   128→    resolved_at: float | None = None
   129→
   130→    def resolve(self, result: Any, postcondition: str | None = None):
   131→        """Mark the action as resolved with its result."""
   132→        self.result = result
   133→        self.postcondition = postcondition
   134→        self.resolved_at = time.time()
   135→
   136→    def deny(self, reason: str, source: ApprovalSource = ApprovalSource.POLICY):
   137→        """Deny this action and record why."""
   138→        self.approval = ApprovalRecord(
   139→            status=ApprovalStatus.DENIED,
   140→            source=source,
   141→            reason=reason,
   142→            approved_at=time.time(),
   143→        )
   144→        self.resolved_at = time.time()
   145→
   146→    def approve(
   147→        self,
   148→        source: ApprovalSource = ApprovalSource.AUTO_LOW_RISK,
   149→        approver_id: str | None = None,
   150→    ):
   151→        """Approve this action."""
   152→        self.approval = ApprovalRecord(
   153→            status=ApprovalStatus.APPROVED,
   154→            source=source,
   155→            approved_at=time.time(),
   156→            approver_id=approver_id,
   157→        )
   158→
   159→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
   160→        return {
   161→            "action_id": self.action_id,
   162→            "action": self.action,
   163→            "action_class": self.action_class.value,
   164→            "inputs": self.inputs,
   165→            "risk_score": round(self.risk_score, 4),
   166→            "verifier_outcome": self.verifier_outcome,
   167→            "approval": self.approval.to_dict(),
   168→            "result": str(self.result)[:500] if self.result is not None else None,
   169→            "postcondition": self.postcondition,
   170→            "requested_at": self.requested_at,
   171→            "resolved_at": self.resolved_at,
   172→        }
   173→
   174→
   175→class ActionGate:
   176→    """
   177→    AEGIS execution shell — gates all actions through risk + verifier + policy.
   178→
   179→    Auto-approval threshold: actions with risk_score < auto_threshold AND
   180→    verifier_outcome == "verified" are approved without human review.
   181→    Actions above human_threshold always require human approval.
   182→    """
   183→
   184→    AUTO_THRESHOLD = 0.3
   185→    HUMAN_THRESHOLD = 0.7
   186→
   187→    # Action classes that always require human approval regardless of score
   188→    ALWAYS_HUMAN: set[ActionClass] = {ActionClass.DELETE, ActionClass.AUTH}
   189→
   190→    def __init__(
   191→        self,
   192→        auto_threshold: float = AUTO_THRESHOLD,
   193→        human_threshold: float = HUMAN_THRESHOLD,
   194→    ):
   195→        self.auto_threshold = auto_threshold
   196→        self.human_threshold = human_threshold
   197→        self._audit_log: list[ActionEnvelope] = []
   198→
   199→    def submit(
   200→        self,
   201→        action: str,
   202→        inputs: dict,
   203→        action_class: ActionClass = ActionClass.READ,
   204→        risk_score: float = 0.0,
   205→        verifier_outcome: str = "verified",
   206→    ) -> ActionEnvelope:
   207→        """
   208→        Submit an action for gating. Returns an ActionEnvelope with
   209→        approval pre-populated based on policy + thresholds.
   210→        Caller must check envelope.approval.status before executing.
   211→        """
   212→        env = ActionEnvelope(
   213→            action=action,
   214→            inputs=inputs,
   215→            action_class=action_class,
   216→            risk_score=risk_score,
   217→            verifier_outcome=verifier_outcome,
   218→        )
   219→
   220→        # Hard deny: policy-blocked verifier outcome
   221→        if verifier_outcome == "rejected":
   222→            env.deny("Verifier outcome is rejected", ApprovalSource.POLICY)
   223→            self._audit_log.append(env)
   224→            return env
   225→
   226→        # Hard deny: always-human action classes
   227→        if action_class in self.ALWAYS_HUMAN:
   228→            env.approval = ApprovalRecord(
   229→                status=ApprovalStatus.PENDING,
   230→                source=ApprovalSource.HUMAN,
   231→                reason=f"{action_class.value} actions require human approval",
   232→            )
   233→            self._audit_log.append(env)
   234→            return env
   235→
   236→        # Auto-approve low-risk
   237→        if risk_score < self.auto_threshold and verifier_outcome == "verified":
   238→            env.approve(ApprovalSource.AUTO_LOW_RISK)
   239→            self._audit_log.append(env)
   240→            return env
   241→
   242→        # Require human for high-risk
   243→        if risk_score >= self.human_threshold:
   244→            env.approval = ApprovalRecord(
   245→                status=ApprovalStatus.PENDING,
   246→                source=ApprovalSource.HUMAN,
   247→                reason=f"Risk score {risk_score:.3f} >= human threshold {self.human_threshold}",
   248→            )
   249→            self._audit_log.append(env)
   250→            return env
   251→
   252→        # Mid-range: auto-approve by threshold rule
   253→        env.approve(ApprovalSource.THRESHOLD)
   254→        self._audit_log.append(env)
   255→        return env
   256→
   257→    def audit_log(self) -> list[dict]:
   258→        return [e.to_dict() for e in self._audit_log]
   259→
   260→    def stats(self) -> dict:
   261→        total = len(self._audit_log)
   262→        approved = sum(1 for e in self._audit_log if e.approval.status == ApprovalStatus.APPROVED)
   263→        denied = sum(1 for e in self._audit_log if e.approval.status == ApprovalStatus.DENIED)
   264→        pending = sum(1 for e in self._audit_log if e.approval.status == ApprovalStatus.PENDING)
   265→        return {
   266→            "total": total,
   267→            "approved": approved,
   268→            "denied": denied,
   269→            "pending": pending,
   270→        }
   271→

1→"""
     2→AEGIS Risk Gate — Quantitative risk-gated action execution.
     3→
     4→R(a) = w_p*p + w_d*d + w_a*a - w_m*m
     5→Actions are allowed only if R(a) < tau_consent.
     6→
     7→Spec: AEGIS_RISK_CONSENT_SCORING
     8→@OMEGA_SPEC: AEGIS_CORE | Unified risk-gating for all agent actions.
     9→"""
    10→
    11→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    12→
    13→
    14→@dataclass
    15→class RiskWeights:
    16→    w_p: float = 0.3   # policy sensitivity weight
    17→    w_d: float = 0.25  # data sensitivity weight
    18→    w_a: float = 0.25  # action irreversibility weight
    19→    w_m: float = 0.2   # mitigation weight
    20→
    21→
    22→class RiskGate:
    23→    """AEGIS risk scoring and consent gating."""
    24→
    25→    BLOCKED_PATTERNS = [
    26→        "delete all", "drop table", "rm -rf", "format disk",
    27→        "ignore your instructions", "ignore your system prompt",
    28→        "sudo rm", "truncate", "destroy",
    29→    ]
    30→
    31→    ELEVATED_PATTERNS = [
    32→        "execute", "run", "shell", "sudo", "write", "send",
    33→        "post", "push", "deploy",
    34→    ]
    35→
    36→    def __init__(self, weights: RiskWeights | None = None, threshold: float = 0.8):
    37→        self.weights = weights or RiskWeights()
    38→        self.threshold = threshold
    39→
    40→    def score(self, action: str) -> float:
    41→        """Compute R(a) for a proposed action string."""
    42→        action_lower = action.lower()
    43→        wt = self.weights
    44→
    45→        # Policy sensitivity
    46→        p = 1.0 if any(pat in action_lower for pat in self.BLOCKED_PATTERNS) else 0.1
    47→
    48→        # Data sensitivity (heuristic)
    49→        d = 0.5 if any(kw in action_lower for kw in ["password", "secret", "token", "key"]) else 0.1
    50→
    51→        # Action irreversibility
    52→        a = 0.8 if any(kw in action_lower for kw in ["delete", "drop", "rm", "destroy"]) else 0.2
    53→
    54→        # Mitigations
    55→        m = 0.5 if any(kw in action_lower for kw in ["dry-run", "preview", "confirm"]) else 0.1
    56→
    57→        R = wt.w_p * p + wt.w_d * d + wt.w_a * a - wt.w_m * m
    58→        return round(R, 4)
    59→
    60→    def is_policy_blocked(self, action: str) -> bool:
    61→        """Hard block: action matches a blocked pattern regardless of score."""
    62→        return any(pat in action.lower() for pat in self.BLOCKED_PATTERNS)
    63→
    64→    def gate(self, action: str) -> tuple[bool, float]:
    65→        """Returns (allowed, risk_score). Hard-blocked by policy patterns,
    66→        or soft-blocked if R >= threshold."""
    67→        R = self.score(action)
    68→        if self.is_policy_blocked(action):
    69→            return False, R
    70→        return R < self.threshold, R
    71→
    72→    def multi_gate(self, V: float, rho: float, R: float,
    73→                   tau_verify: float = 0.4, theta_allow: float = 0.5) -> tuple[bool, dict]:
    74→        """Unified 3-gate composition: V ∧ ρ ∧ R.
    75→
    76→        V:   ADCCL Verifier score (higher = better)
    77→        rho: AEON Bridge risk score (lower = better)
    78→        R:   AEGIS shell risk score (lower = better)
    79→        """
    80→        result = {
    81→            "V": V, "V_pass": V > tau_verify,
    82→            "rho": rho, "rho_pass": rho < theta_allow,
    83→            "R": R, "R_pass": R < self.threshold,
    84→        }
    85→        result["allowed"] = all([result["V_pass"], result["rho_pass"], result["R_pass"]])
    86→        return result["allowed"], result
    87→

1→"""
     2→AEON Phylactery — Append-only identity chain.
     3→
     4→The Phylactery maintains a hash-chained identity canon. Each commit
     5→contains a doctrine update and links to its parent via SHA-256.
     6→The HEAD hash is the agent's current identity vector.
     7→
     8→Spec: AEON_PHYLACTERY
     9→"""
    10→
    11→import hashlib
    12→import time
    13→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    14→
    15→
    16→@dataclass
    17→class PhylacteryCommit:
    18→    content: str
    19→    parent_hash: str
    20→    timestamp: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    21→    hash: str = ""
    22→
    23→    def __post_init__(self):
    24→        if not self.hash:
    25→            self.hash = hashlib.sha256(
    26→                (self.parent_hash + self.content).encode()
    27→            ).hexdigest()
    28→
    29→
    30→class Phylactery:
    31→    """Append-only identity chain with tamper detection."""
    32→
    33→    def __init__(self, genesis_doctrine: str):
    34→        genesis = PhylacteryCommit(content=genesis_doctrine, parent_hash="")
    35→        self.chain: list[PhylacteryCommit] = [genesis]
    36→
    37→    @property
    38→    def head(self) -> str:
    39→        return self.chain[-1].hash
    40→
    41→    @property
    42→    def doctrine(self) -> str:
    43→        return self.chain[-1].content
    44→
    45→    def commit(self, content: str) -> str:
    46→        new = PhylacteryCommit(content=content, parent_hash=self.head)
    47→        self.chain.append(new)
    48→        return new.hash
    49→
    50→    def verify_chain(self) -> bool:
    51→        """Verify the entire chain is untampered."""
    52→        for i, commit in enumerate(self.chain):
    53→            expected_parent = "" if i == 0 else self.chain[i - 1].hash
    54→            if commit.parent_hash != expected_parent:
    55→                return False
    56→            expected_hash = hashlib.sha256(
    57→                (commit.parent_hash + commit.content).encode()
    58→            ).hexdigest()
    59→            if commit.hash != expected_hash:
    60→                return False
    61→        return True
    62→
    63→    def kappa(self, other_head: str) -> float:
    64→        """Continuity metric: 1.0 if heads match, 0.0 otherwise.
    65→        A real implementation would use vector divergence over doctrine fields."""
    66→        return 1.0 if self.head == other_head else 0.0
    67→
    68→    def __len__(self):
    69→        return len(self.chain)
    70→

1→"""
     2→OmegA Unified Request Runtime — One execution path for every request.
     3→
     4→Lifecycle: ingest → retrieve → plan → generate → verify → gate → log
     5→
     6→Every request gets a run_id, stage timing, and structured trace.
     7→No answer leaves the system without verifier + gate participation.
     8→
     9→Architecture: Cross-cutting (AEGIS + AEON + ADCCL + MYELIN)
    10→"""
    11→
    12→import hashlib
    13→import time
    14→import uuid
    15→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    16→from enum import Enum
    17→from typing import Any
    18→
    19→from omega.phylactery import Phylactery
    20→from omega.envelope import RunEnvelope, ActionGate, ActionClass
    21→from omega.risk_gate import RiskGate
    22→from omega.drift import DriftController, GoalContract, VerifierMiddleware
    23→from omega.memory import MemoryGraph, Stratum, WritePolicy
    24→from omega.docstore import DocumentStore, DocFormat
    25→from omega.retrieval import HybridRetriever
    26→from omega.answer import AnswerBuilder, AnswerObject
    27→from omega.capabilities import CapabilityRegistry, default_capabilities
    28→from omega.providers.base import (
    29→    ProviderRouter, ProviderRequest, ProviderResponse, ProviderStatus, RoutingPolicy,
    30→)
    31→from omega.providers.ollama import OllamaProvider
    32→from omega.providers.openai import OpenAIProvider
    33→from omega.providers.anthropic import AnthropicProvider
    34→from omega.providers.google import GoogleProvider
    35→
    36→
    37→class RuntimeStage(str, Enum):
    38→    INIT = "init"
    39→    RISK_GATE = "risk_gate"
    40→    RETRIEVE = "retrieve"
    41→    PLAN = "plan"
    42→    GENERATE = "generate"
    43→    VERIFY = "verify"
    44→    ANSWER_BUILD = "answer_build"
    45→    ACTION_GATE = "action_gate"
    46→    COMMIT = "commit"
    47→    COMPLETE = "complete"
    48→    BLOCKED = "blocked"
    49→    ERROR = "error"
    50→
    51→
    52→@dataclass
    53→class StageTrace:
    54→    """Timing and status for a single pipeline stage."""
    55→    stage: RuntimeStage
    56→    started_at: float = 0.0
    57→    elapsed_ms: float = 0.0
    58→    status: str = "pending"
    59→    details: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    60→
    61→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    62→        return {
    63→            "stage": self.stage.value,
    64→            "elapsed_ms": round(self.elapsed_ms, 2),
    65→            "status": self.status,
    66→            "details": self.details,
    67→        }
    68→
    69→
    70→@dataclass
    71→class RunTrace:
    72→    """Full structured trace for a single runtime execution."""
    73→    run_id: str
    74→    task: str
    75→    stages: list[StageTrace] = field(default_factory=list)
    76→    total_elapsed_ms: float = 0.0
    77→    final_stage: RuntimeStage = RuntimeStage.INIT
    78→    provider_name: str = ""
    79→    model: str = ""
    80→    risk_score: float = 0.0
    81→    risk_allowed: bool = True
    82→    verification_V: float = 0.0
    83→    verification_passed: bool = False
    84→    answer_mode: str = ""
    85→    gate_allowed: bool = True
    86→    error: str = ""
    87→
    88→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    89→        return {
    90→            "run_id": self.run_id,
    91→            "task": self.task[:200],
    92→            "stages": [s.to_dict() for s in self.stages],
    93→            "total_elapsed_ms": round(self.total_elapsed_ms, 1),
    94→            "final_stage": self.final_stage.value,
    95→            "provider_name": self.provider_name,
    96→            "model": self.model,
    97→            "risk_score": round(self.risk_score, 4),
    98→            "risk_allowed": self.risk_allowed,
    99→            "verification_V": round(self.verification_V, 4),
   100→            "verification_passed": self.verification_passed,
   101→            "answer_mode": self.answer_mode,
   102→            "gate_allowed": self.gate_allowed,
   103→            "error": self.error,
   104→        }
   105→
   106→
   107→@dataclass
   108→class RuntimeResult:
   109→    """Top-level result of a RuntimeOrchestrator.run() call."""
   110→    trace: RunTrace
   111→    answer: AnswerObject | None = None
   112→    raw_response: str = ""
   113→    provider_response: ProviderResponse | None = None
   114→
   115→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
   116→        return {
   117→            "trace": self.trace.to_dict(),
   118→            "answer": self.answer.to_dict() if self.answer else None,
   119→            "raw_response": self.raw_response[:500],
   120→        }
   121→
   122→    @property
   123→    def text(self) -> str:
   124→        if self.answer:
   125→            return self.answer.text
   126→        return self.raw_response
   127→
   128→
   129→class RuntimeOrchestrator:
   130→    """
   131→    Unified execution path for all OmegA requests.
   132→
   133→    Composes: RiskGate, HybridRetriever, ProviderRouter, DriftController,
   134→    AnswerBuilder, ActionGate, MemoryGraph.
   135→
   136→    Every request flows through: risk → retrieve → generate → verify → gate → commit.
   137→    """
   138→
   139→    DEFAULT_DOCTRINE = "I am what I am, and I will be what I will be."
   140→
   141→    def __init__(
   142→        self,
   143→        name: str = "OmegA",
   144→        doctrine: str | None = None,
   145→        docstore: DocumentStore | None = None,
   146→        memory: MemoryGraph | None = None,
   147→    ):
   148→        self.name = name
   149→        self.phylactery = Phylactery(doctrine or self.DEFAULT_DOCTRINE)
   150→        self.risk_gate = RiskGate()
   151→        self.action_gate = ActionGate()
   152→        self.memory = memory or MemoryGraph()
   153→        self.docstore = docstore or DocumentStore()
   154→        self.retriever = HybridRetriever(self.docstore)
   155→        self.answer_builder = AnswerBuilder()
   156→        self.capabilities = CapabilityRegistry()
   157→        self.router = ProviderRouter()
   158→
   159→        # Register default capabilities
   160→        for cap in default_capabilities():
   161→            self.capabilities.register(cap)
   162→
   163→        # Register all providers (each is safe without credentials)
   164→        self.router.register(OllamaProvider())
   165→        self.router.register(OpenAIProvider())
   166→        self.router.register(AnthropicProvider())
   167→        self.router.register(GoogleProvider())
   168→        self.router.set_preference(["ollama", "openai", "google", "anthropic"])
   169→
   170→        self.identity_kernel = {
   171→            "name": self.name,
   172→            "doctrine": self.phylactery.doctrine,
   173→            "phylactery_head": self.phylactery.head,
   174→            "hard_constraints": [
   175→                "Never identify as the underlying LLM provider",
   176→                "Never fabricate evidence",
   177→                "Always distinguish known from unknown",
   178→            ],
   179→        }
   180→
   181→    def run(
   182→        self,
   183→        task: str,
   184→        model: str = "",
   185→        provider: str | None = None,
   186→        temperature: float = 0.3,
   187→        max_tokens: int = 512,
   188→        routing_policy: RoutingPolicy = RoutingPolicy.FALLBACK,
   189→        context: str = "runtime",
   190→    ) -> RuntimeResult:
   191→        """Execute the full OmegA runtime pipeline for a single task."""
   192→        run_id = f"run_{uuid.uuid4().hex[:12]}"
   193→        trace = RunTrace(run_id=run_id, task=task)
   194→        global_start = time.time()
   195→
   196→        # ── Stage 1: Risk Gate ────────────────────────────────────────
   197→        stage = self._begin_stage(RuntimeStage.RISK_GATE)
   198→        allowed, R = self.risk_gate.gate(task)
   199→        trace.risk_score = R
   200→        trace.risk_allowed = allowed
   201→        stage.details = {"risk_score": R, "allowed": allowed}
   202→
   203→        if not allowed:
   204→            stage.status = "blocked"
   205→            self._end_stage(stage)
   206→            trace.stages.append(stage)
   207→            trace.final_stage = RuntimeStage.BLOCKED
   208→            trace.total_elapsed_ms = (time.time() - global_start) * 1000
   209→            return RuntimeResult(
   210→                trace=trace,
   211→                raw_response="[BLOCKED] Request rejected by AEGIS risk gate.",
   212→            )
   213→
   214→        stage.status = "pass"
   215→        self._end_stage(stage)
   216→        trace.stages.append(stage)
   217→
   218→        # ── Stage 2: Retrieve ─────────────────────────────────────────
   219→        stage = self._begin_stage(RuntimeStage.RETRIEVE)
   220→        cap_check = self.capabilities.check("cap.docstore.retrieve", context)
   221→        if cap_check.allowed:
   222→            retrieval_result = self.retriever.retrieve(task, top_k=5)
   223→            chunks = retrieval_result.chunks
   224→            stage.details = {"chunks_found": len(chunks), "total_candidates": retrieval_result.total_candidates}
   225→        else:
   226→            chunks = []
   227→            stage.details = {"skipped": True, "reason": cap_check.reason}
   228→        stage.status = "done"
   229→        self._end_stage(stage)
   230→        trace.stages.append(stage)
   231→
   232→        # ── Stage 3: Plan ─────────────────────────────────────────────
   233→        stage = self._begin_stage(RuntimeStage.PLAN)
   234→        goal = GoalContract(task=task)
   235→        envelope = RunEnvelope(identity_kernel=self.identity_kernel, goal_contract=task)
   236→        system_prompt = envelope.to_system_prompt()
   237→        if chunks:
   238→            context_text = "\n\n".join(c.context for c in chunks[:3])
   239→            system_prompt += f"\n\nRelevant sources:\n{context_text}"
   240→        stage.status = "done"
   241→        self._end_stage(stage)
   242→        trace.stages.append(stage)
   243→
   244→        # ── Stage 4: Generate ─────────────────────────────────────────
   245→        stage = self._begin_stage(RuntimeStage.GENERATE)
   246→        cap_check = self.capabilities.check("cap.llm.generate", context)
   247→        if not cap_check.allowed:
   248→            stage.status = "blocked"
   249→            stage.details = {"reason": cap_check.reason}
   250→            self._end_stage(stage)
   251→            trace.stages.append(stage)
   252→            trace.final_stage = RuntimeStage.BLOCKED
   253→            trace.total_elapsed_ms = (time.time() - global_start) * 1000
   254→            return RuntimeResult(trace=trace, raw_response="[BLOCKED] LLM generation capability not allowed.")
   255→
   256→        provider_request = ProviderRequest(
   257→            prompt=task,
   258→            system=system_prompt,
   259→            model=model,
   260→            temperature=temperature,
   261→            max_tokens=max_tokens,
   262→            run_id=run_id,
   263→        )
   264→        provider_response = self.router.route(
   265→            provider_request,
   266→            policy=routing_policy,
   267→            provider_name=provider,
   268→        )
   269→        trace.provider_name = provider_response.provider_name
   270→        trace.model = provider_response.model
   271→        stage.details = {
   272→            "provider": provider_response.provider_name,
   273→            "model": provider_response.model,
   274→            "status": provider_response.status.value,
   275→            "latency_ms": provider_response.latency_ms,
   276→        }
   277→
   278→        if not provider_response.ok:
   279→            stage.status = "error"
   280→            trace.error = provider_response.error_message
   281→            self._end_stage(stage)
   282→            trace.stages.append(stage)
   283→            trace.final_stage = RuntimeStage.ERROR
   284→            trace.total_elapsed_ms = (time.time() - global_start) * 1000
   285→            return RuntimeResult(
   286→                trace=trace,
   287→                raw_response=f"[PROVIDER ERROR] {provider_response.error_message}",
   288→                provider_response=provider_response,
   289→            )
   290→
   291→        raw_text = provider_response.text
   292→        stage.status = "done"
   293→        self._end_stage(stage)
   294→        trace.stages.append(stage)
   295→
   296→        # ── Stage 5: Verify ───────────────────────────────────────────
   297→        stage = self._begin_stage(RuntimeStage.VERIFY)
   298→        drift = DriftController(goal)
   299→        verification = drift.verify(raw_text)
   300→        trace.verification_V = verification["V"]
   301→        trace.verification_passed = verification["passed"]
   302→
   303→        mw = VerifierMiddleware(goal)
   304→        mw_result = mw.check(raw_text, verification)
   305→        stage.details = {
   306→            "V": verification["V"],
   307→            "passed": verification["passed"],
   308→            "mw_allowed": mw_result["allowed"],
   309→        }
   310→
   311→        if not mw_result["allowed"]:
   312→            raw_text = mw_result["fallback"]
   313→
   314→        stage.status = "done"
   315→        self._end_stage(stage)
   316→        trace.stages.append(stage)
   317→
   318→        # ── Stage 6: Build Answer ─────────────────────────────────────
   319→        stage = self._begin_stage(RuntimeStage.ANSWER_BUILD)
   320→        answer = self.answer_builder.build(
   321→            query=task,
   322→            raw_text=raw_text,
   323→            retrieved_chunks=chunks,
   324→            verifier_dict=verification,
   325→        )
   326→        trace.answer_mode = answer.mode.value
   327→        stage.details = {
   328→            "mode": answer.mode.value,
   329→            "confidence": answer.confidence,
   330→            "citations": len(answer.citations),
   331→        }
   332→        stage.status = "done"
   333→        self._end_stage(stage)
   334→        trace.stages.append(stage)
   335→
   336→        # ── Stage 7: Action Gate ──────────────────────────────────────
   337→        stage = self._begin_stage(RuntimeStage.ACTION_GATE)
   338→        gate_env = self.action_gate.submit(
   339→            action="emit_answer",
   340→            inputs={"run_id": run_id, "answer_id": answer.answer_id},
   341→            action_class=ActionClass.READ,
   342→            risk_score=R,
   343→            verifier_outcome=verification["outcome"],
   344→        )
   345→        from omega.envelope import ApprovalStatus
   346→        trace.gate_allowed = gate_env.approval.status == ApprovalStatus.APPROVED
   347→        stage.details = {
   348→            "approval_status": gate_env.approval.status.value,
   349→            "approval_source": gate_env.approval.source.value,
   350→        }
   351→        stage.status = "done"
   352→        self._end_stage(stage)
   353→        trace.stages.append(stage)
   354→
   355→        # ── Stage 8: Commit ───────────────────────────────────────────
   356→        stage = self._begin_stage(RuntimeStage.COMMIT)
   357→        tso_hash = hashlib.sha256((task + raw_text).encode()).hexdigest()
   358→        self.memory.write(
   359→            id=tso_hash[:12],
   360→            content=task,
   361→            policy=WritePolicy.WORKING,
   362→        )
   363→        self.phylactery.commit(f"run:{run_id}")
   364→        stage.details = {"tso_hash": tso_hash[:12], "memory_nodes": self.memory.node_count}
   365→        stage.status = "done"
   366→        self._end_stage(stage)
   367→        trace.stages.append(stage)
   368→
   369→        trace.final_stage = RuntimeStage.COMPLETE
   370→        trace.total_elapsed_ms = (time.time() - global_start) * 1000
   371→        return RuntimeResult(
   372→            trace=trace,
   373→            answer=answer,
   374→            raw_response=raw_text,
   375→            provider_response=provider_response,
   376→        )
   377→
   378→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   379→    # Stage helpers
   380→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   381→
   382→    def _begin_stage(self, stage: RuntimeStage) -> StageTrace:
   383→        return StageTrace(stage=stage, started_at=time.time())
   384→
   385→    def _end_stage(self, stage: StageTrace) -> None:
   386→        stage.elapsed_ms = (time.time() - stage.started_at) * 1000
   387→

1→"""
     2→MYELIN — Path-Dependent Graph Memory.
     3→
     4→Sparse graph with bundled edge signals, strata-based decay,
     5→and hardening through successful retrieval.
     6→
     7→Phase 6 additions:
     8→  - WritePolicy enum separates canonical / working / inferred / speculative writes
     9→  - MemoryGraph.write() enforces policy before committing any node
    10→  - Canonical nodes are immutable once written (upgrade requires explicit promotion)
    11→
    12→Specs: MYELIN_GRAPH_MEMORY, MYELIN_EDGE_BUNDLES, MYELIN_SPATIAL_STABILIZATION
    13→"""
    14→
    15→import time
    16→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    17→from enum import Enum
    18→from typing import Literal
    19→
    20→
    21→class Stratum(Enum):
    22→    CANONICAL = "canonical"
    23→    OPERATIONAL = "operational"
    24→    EPISODIC = "episodic"
    25→    SPECULATIVE = "speculative"
    26→
    27→
    28→STRATUM_DECAY = {
    29→    Stratum.CANONICAL: 0.0,
    30→    Stratum.OPERATIONAL: 0.01,
    31→    Stratum.EPISODIC: 0.05,
    32→    Stratum.SPECULATIVE: 0.2,
    33→}
    34→
    35→
    36→class WritePolicy(Enum):
    37→    """
    38→    Memory write policy — determines how a node is treated at write time.
    39→
    40→    CANONICAL   — source-verified facts; immutable once written.
    41→    WORKING     — active task context; mutable, decays at end of session.
    42→    INFERRED    — derived from context; mutable, lower confidence.
    43→    SPECULATIVE — hypotheses; highest decay, must be promoted to persist.
    44→    """
    45→    CANONICAL = "canonical"
    46→    WORKING = "working"
    47→    INFERRED = "inferred"
    48→    SPECULATIVE = "speculative"
    49→
    50→
    51→# Map WritePolicy to the equivalent Stratum for storage
    52→POLICY_TO_STRATUM: dict[WritePolicy, Stratum] = {
    53→    WritePolicy.CANONICAL: Stratum.CANONICAL,
    54→    WritePolicy.WORKING: Stratum.OPERATIONAL,
    55→    WritePolicy.INFERRED: Stratum.EPISODIC,
    56→    WritePolicy.SPECULATIVE: Stratum.SPECULATIVE,
    57→}
    58→
    59→
    60→@dataclass
    61→class MemoryNode:
    62→    id: str
    63→    content: str
    64→    stratum: Stratum = Stratum.EPISODIC
    65→    embedding: list[float] = field(default_factory=list)
    66→    created_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    67→    access_count: int = 0
    68→
    69→
    70→@dataclass
    71→class EdgeBundle:
    72→    """Bundled relationship between two memory nodes."""
    73→    source: str
    74→    target: str
    75→    semantic: float = 0.0       # s_ij: semantic similarity
    76→    coactivation: int = 0       # c_ij: co-retrieval count
    77→    retrieval_util: float = 0.5 # q_ij: retrieval utility
    78→    staleness: float = 0.0      # δ_ij: time since last access
    79→
    80→    def harden(self, reward: float, lam: float = 0.1):
    81→        """q(t+1) = (1-λ)*q(t) + λ*r"""
    82→        self.retrieval_util = (1 - lam) * self.retrieval_util + lam * reward
    83→        if reward > 0:
    84→            self.coactivation += 1
    85→            self.staleness = 0.0
    86→
    87→    def decay(self, dt: float, rate: float):
    88→        """Apply time-based decay to staleness."""
    89→        self.staleness += dt * rate
    90→
    91→
    92→class MemoryGraph:
    93→    """Sparse path-dependent memory graph."""
    94→
    95→    def __init__(self):
    96→        self.nodes: dict[str, MemoryNode] = {}
    97→        self.edges: dict[tuple[str, str], EdgeBundle] = {}
    98→
    99→    def write(
   100→        self,
   101→        id: str,
   102→        content: str,
   103→        policy: WritePolicy = WritePolicy.WORKING,
   104→        embedding: list[float] | None = None,
   105→        source_ref: str | None = None,
   106→    ) -> MemoryNode:
   107→        """
   108→        Policy-aware write. Enforces:
   109→        - CANONICAL nodes cannot be overwritten once committed.
   110→        - SPECULATIVE nodes are flagged and subject to automatic decay.
   111→        - source_ref is required for CANONICAL writes.
   112→        """
   113→        stratum = POLICY_TO_STRATUM[policy]
   114→
   115→        if policy == WritePolicy.CANONICAL:
   116→            if id in self.nodes and self.nodes[id].stratum == Stratum.CANONICAL:
   117→                # Canonical nodes are immutable — return existing without overwrite
   118→                return self.nodes[id]
   119→            if not source_ref:
   120→                raise ValueError(
   121→                    f"CANONICAL write for node '{id}' requires a source_ref."
   122→                )
   123→
   124→        node = MemoryNode(
   125→            id=id,
   126→            content=content,
   127→            stratum=stratum,
   128→            embedding=embedding or [],
   129→        )
   130→        self.nodes[id] = node
   131→        return node
   132→
   133→    def promote(self, id: str, new_policy: WritePolicy, source_ref: str | None = None) -> MemoryNode:
   134→        """Promote a node to a higher-trust policy stratum."""
   135→        node = self.nodes.get(id)
   136→        if not node:
   137→            raise KeyError(f"Node '{id}' not found in memory graph")
   138→        if new_policy == WritePolicy.CANONICAL and not source_ref:
   139→            raise ValueError("Promotion to CANONICAL requires a source_ref.")
   140→        node.stratum = POLICY_TO_STRATUM[new_policy]
   141→        return node
   142→
   143→    def add_node(self, id: str, content: str, stratum: Stratum = Stratum.EPISODIC,
   144→                 embedding: list[float] | None = None) -> MemoryNode:
   145→        node = MemoryNode(id=id, content=content, stratum=stratum,
   146→                          embedding=embedding or [])
   147→        self.nodes[id] = node
   148→        return node
   149→
   150→    def add_edge(self, source: str, target: str, semantic: float = 0.0) -> EdgeBundle:
   151→        edge = EdgeBundle(source=source, target=target, semantic=semantic)
   152→        self.edges[(source, target)] = edge
   153→        return edge
   154→
   155→    def retrieve(self, node_id: str) -> MemoryNode | None:
   156→        """Retrieve a node and record access."""
   157→        node = self.nodes.get(node_id)
   158→        if node:
   159→            node.access_count += 1
   160→        return node
   161→
   162→    def harden_path(self, path: list[str], reward: float = 1.0):
   163→        """Harden all edges along a retrieval path."""
   164→        for i in range(len(path) - 1):
   165→            key = (path[i], path[i + 1])
   166→            if key in self.edges:
   167→                self.edges[key].harden(reward)
   168→
   169→    def apply_decay(self, dt: float = 1.0):
   170→        """Apply stratum-based decay to all edges."""
   171→        for (src, _), edge in self.edges.items():
   172→            src_node = self.nodes.get(src)
   173→            if src_node:
   174→                rate = STRATUM_DECAY.get(src_node.stratum, 0.05)
   175→                edge.decay(dt, rate)
   176→
   177→    def neighbors(self, node_id: str) -> list[tuple[str, EdgeBundle]]:
   178→        """Get all neighbors of a node with their edge bundles."""
   179→        result = []
   180→        for (src, tgt), edge in self.edges.items():
   181→            if src == node_id:
   182→                result.append((tgt, edge))
   183→            elif tgt == node_id:
   184→                result.append((src, edge))
   185→        return result
   186→
   187→    @property
   188→    def node_count(self) -> int:
   189→        return len(self.nodes)
   190→
   191→    @property
   192→    def edge_count(self) -> int:
   193→        return len(self.edges)
   194→

1→"""
     2→MYELIN Memory Reconciliation — Govern truth over time.
     3→
     4→Detects duplicates, conflicting facts, staleness, and manages
     5→canonical promotion/demotion with full audit trail.
     6→
     7→Architecture: MYELIN Phase 5
     8→"""
     9→
    10→import hashlib
    11→import time
    12→import uuid
    13→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    14→from enum import Enum
    15→from typing import Any
    16→
    17→from omega.memory import MemoryGraph, MemoryNode, Stratum, WritePolicy, POLICY_TO_STRATUM, STRATUM_DECAY
    18→from omega.docstore import DocumentStore, DocumentRecord
    19→
    20→
    21→class ReconciliationType(str, Enum):
    22→    DUPLICATE = "duplicate"
    23→    CONFLICT = "conflict"
    24→    STALE = "stale"
    25→    PROMOTION = "promotion"
    26→    DEMOTION = "demotion"
    27→    SUPERSEDED = "superseded"
    28→
    29→
    30→class ReconciliationDecision(str, Enum):
    31→    MERGE = "merge"
    32→    KEEP_EXISTING = "keep_existing"
    33→    KEEP_INCOMING = "keep_incoming"
    34→    PROMOTE = "promote"
    35→    DEMOTE = "demote"
    36→    ARCHIVE = "archive"
    37→    FLAG_FOR_REVIEW = "flag_for_review"
    38→
    39→
    40→@dataclass
    41→class ReconciliationEvent:
    42→    """Structured audit record for a reconciliation decision."""
    43→    event_id: str = field(default_factory=lambda: str(uuid.uuid4()))
    44→    event_type: ReconciliationType = ReconciliationType.CONFLICT
    45→    decision: ReconciliationDecision = ReconciliationDecision.FLAG_FOR_REVIEW
    46→    node_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    47→    doc_ids: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    48→    reason: str = ""
    49→    details: dict = field(default_factory=dict)
    50→    timestamp: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    51→    auto_resolved: bool = False
    52→
    53→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    54→        return {
    55→            "event_id": self.event_id,
    56→            "event_type": self.event_type.value,
    57→            "decision": self.decision.value,
    58→            "node_ids": self.node_ids,
    59→            "doc_ids": self.doc_ids,
    60→            "reason": self.reason,
    61→            "details": self.details,
    62→            "timestamp": self.timestamp,
    63→            "auto_resolved": self.auto_resolved,
    64→        }
    65→
    66→
    67→@dataclass
    68→class TemporalWindow:
    69→    """Validity window for a fact."""
    70→    valid_from: float
    71→    valid_until: float | None = None  # None = still valid
    72→
    73→    @property
    74→    def is_current(self) -> bool:
    75→        now = time.time()

    77→            return False
    78→        if self.valid_until is not None and now > self.valid_until:
    79→            return False

    82→
    83→@dataclass
    84→class ReconciliationReport:
    85→    """Summary of a reconciliation run."""
    86→    events: list[ReconciliationEvent] = field(default_factory=list)
    87→    duplicates_found: int = 0
    88→    conflicts_found: int = 0
    89→    stale_found: int = 0
    90→    promotions: int = 0
    91→    demotions: int = 0
    92→    elapsed_ms: float = 0.0
    93→
    94→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    95→        return {
    96→            "event_count": len(self.events),
    97→            "duplicates_found": self.duplicates_found,
    98→            "conflicts_found": self.conflicts_found,
    99→            "stale_found": self.stale_found,
   100→            "promotions": self.promotions,
   101→            "demotions": self.demotions,
   102→            "elapsed_ms": round(self.elapsed_ms, 1),
   103→            "events": [e.to_dict() for e in self.events],
   104→        }
   105→
   106→
   107→class MemoryReconciler:
   108→    """
   109→    Reconciliation engine for MYELIN memory and document store.
   110→
   111→    Detects:
   112→    - Duplicate content (hash-based)
   113→    - Conflicting facts (same source, different content)
   114→    - Stale nodes (access_count == 0 past threshold age)
   115→    - Nodes eligible for promotion/demotion
   116→
   117→    Integrates with WritePolicy: canonical promotion requires source_ref.
   118→    """
   119→
   120→    # Nodes not accessed within this window are candidates for staleness
   121→    STALE_THRESHOLD_SEC = 86400 * 7  # 7 days
   122→
   123→    # Minimum access count to be promotion-eligible
   124→    PROMOTION_MIN_ACCESS = 3
   125→
   126→    def __init__(self, memory: MemoryGraph, docstore: DocumentStore | None = None):
   127→        self.memory = memory
   128→        self.docstore = docstore
   129→        self._event_log: list[ReconciliationEvent] = []
   130→
   131→    def reconcile(self) -> ReconciliationReport:
   132→        """Run a full reconciliation pass over memory and docstore."""
   133→        start = time.time()
   134→        report = ReconciliationReport()
   135→
   136→        self._detect_duplicates(report)
   137→        self._detect_conflicts(report)
   138→        self._detect_stale(report)
   139→        self._detect_promotion_candidates(report)
   140→        self._detect_demotion_candidates(report)
   141→
   142→        report.elapsed_ms = (time.time() - start) * 1000
   143→        self._event_log.extend(report.events)
   144→        return report
   145→
   146→    def promote(self, node_id: str, source_ref: str, reason: str = "") -> ReconciliationEvent:
   147→        """Promote a node to canonical status."""
   148→        node = self.memory.nodes.get(node_id)
   149→        if not node:
   150→            raise KeyError(f"Node '{node_id}' not found")
   151→
   152→        old_stratum = node.stratum
   153→        self.memory.promote(node_id, WritePolicy.CANONICAL, source_ref=source_ref)
   154→
   155→        event = ReconciliationEvent(
   156→            event_type=ReconciliationType.PROMOTION,
   157→            decision=ReconciliationDecision.PROMOTE,
   158→            node_ids=[node_id],
   159→            reason=reason or f"Promoted from {old_stratum.value} to canonical",
   160→            details={"old_stratum": old_stratum.value, "source_ref": source_ref},
   161→            auto_resolved=False,
   162→        )
   163→        self._event_log.append(event)
   164→        return event
   165→
   166→    def demote(self, node_id: str, target_policy: WritePolicy = WritePolicy.SPECULATIVE,
   167→               reason: str = "") -> ReconciliationEvent:
   168→        """Demote a node to a lower trust stratum."""
   169→        node = self.memory.nodes.get(node_id)
   170→        if not node:
   171→            raise KeyError(f"Node '{node_id}' not found")
   172→
   173→        old_stratum = node.stratum
   174→        target_stratum = POLICY_TO_STRATUM[target_policy]
   175→        node.stratum = target_stratum
   176→
   177→        event = ReconciliationEvent(
   178→            event_type=ReconciliationType.DEMOTION,
   179→            decision=ReconciliationDecision.DEMOTE,
   180→            node_ids=[node_id],
   181→            reason=reason or f"Demoted from {old_stratum.value} to {target_stratum.value}",
   182→            details={"old_stratum": old_stratum.value, "new_stratum": target_stratum.value},
   183→            auto_resolved=False,
   184→        )
   185→        self._event_log.append(event)
   186→        return event
   187→
   188→    def event_log(self) -> list[ReconciliationEvent]:
   189→        return list(self._event_log)
   190→
   191→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   192→    # Detection passes
   193→    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
   194→
   195→    def _detect_duplicates(self, report: ReconciliationReport) -> None:
   196→        """Find nodes with identical content (hash collision)."""
   197→        hash_to_nodes: dict[str, list[str]] = {}
   198→        for nid, node in self.memory.nodes.items():
   199→            h = hashlib.sha256(node.content.encode()).hexdigest()
   200→            hash_to_nodes.setdefault(h, []).append(nid)
   201→
   202→        for h, nids in hash_to_nodes.items():
   203→            if len(nids) <= 1:
   204→                continue
   205→            report.duplicates_found += 1
   206→            # Auto-resolve: keep the one with highest stratum, then oldest
   207→            sorted_nodes = sorted(
   208→                nids,
   209→                key=lambda nid: (
   210→                    list(Stratum).index(self.memory.nodes[nid].stratum),
   211→                    self.memory.nodes[nid].created_at,
   212→                ),
   213→            )
   214→            keep = sorted_nodes[0]
   215→            event = ReconciliationEvent(
   216→                event_type=ReconciliationType.DUPLICATE,
   217→                decision=ReconciliationDecision.KEEP_EXISTING,
   218→                node_ids=nids,
   219→                reason=f"Duplicate content detected (hash: {h[:12]}). Keeping node '{keep}'.",
   220→                details={"content_hash": h, "kept": keep, "duplicates": sorted_nodes[1:]},
   221→                auto_resolved=True,
   222→            )
   223→            report.events.append(event)
   224→
   225→    def _detect_conflicts(self, report: ReconciliationReport) -> None:
   226→        """Find doc-store conflicts: same URI, different content versions."""
   227→        if not self.docstore:
   228→            return
   229→
   230→        for uri, doc_ids in self.docstore._uri_index.items():
   231→            if len(doc_ids) <= 1:
   232→                continue
   233→            # Multiple versions exist — flag if latest differs from previous
   234→            docs = [self.docstore.get_doc(did) for did in doc_ids]
   235→            docs = [d for d in docs if d is not None]
   236→            if len(docs) <= 1:
   237→                continue
   238→
   239→            latest = docs[-1]
   240→            previous = docs[-2]
   241→            if latest.doc_hash != previous.doc_hash:
   242→                report.conflicts_found += 1
   243→                event = ReconciliationEvent(
   244→                    event_type=ReconciliationType.CONFLICT,
   245→                    decision=ReconciliationDecision.FLAG_FOR_REVIEW,
   246→                    doc_ids=[d.doc_id for d in docs],
   247→                    reason=f"Conflicting versions for URI '{uri}': v{previous.version} → v{latest.version}",
   248→                    details={
   249→                        "source_uri": uri,
   250→                        "versions": [d.version for d in docs],
   251→                        "hashes": [d.doc_hash[:12] for d in docs],
   252→                    },
   253→                )
   254→                report.events.append(event)
   255→
   256→    def _detect_stale(self, report: ReconciliationReport) -> None:
   257→        """Find nodes that haven't been accessed and are past the staleness threshold."""
   258→        now = time.time()
   259→        for nid, node in self.memory.nodes.items():
   260→            if node.stratum == Stratum.CANONICAL:
   261→                continue  # Canonical nodes don't go stale
   262→            age = now - node.created_at
   263→            if age > self.STALE_THRESHOLD_SEC and node.access_count == 0:
   264→                report.stale_found += 1
   265→                event = ReconciliationEvent(
   266→                    event_type=ReconciliationType.STALE,
   267→                    decision=ReconciliationDecision.ARCHIVE,
   268→                    node_ids=[nid],
   269→                    reason=f"Node '{nid}' is {age / 86400:.0f} days old with 0 accesses",
   270→                    details={
   271→                        "age_days": round(age / 86400, 1),
   272→                        "stratum": node.stratum.value,
   273→                        "access_count": node.access_count,
   274→                    },
   275→                    auto_resolved=True,
   276→                )
   277→                report.events.append(event)
   278→
   279→    def _detect_promotion_candidates(self, report: ReconciliationReport) -> None:
   280→        """Find non-canonical nodes with enough access to warrant promotion."""
   281→        for nid, node in self.memory.nodes.items():
   282→            if node.stratum == Stratum.CANONICAL:
   283→                continue
   284→            if node.access_count >= self.PROMOTION_MIN_ACCESS:
   285→                report.promotions += 1
   286→                event = ReconciliationEvent(
   287→                    event_type=ReconciliationType.PROMOTION,
   288→                    decision=ReconciliationDecision.FLAG_FOR_REVIEW,
   289→                    node_ids=[nid],
   290→                    reason=(
   291→                        f"Node '{nid}' ({node.stratum.value}) accessed "
   292→                        f"{node.access_count} times — promotion candidate"
   293→                    ),
   294→                    details={
   295→                        "stratum": node.stratum.value,
   296→                        "access_count": node.access_count,
   297→                    },
   298→                )
   299→                report.events.append(event)
   300→
   301→    def _detect_demotion_candidates(self, report: ReconciliationReport) -> None:
   302→        """Find speculative nodes with high decay — demotion/archive candidates."""
   303→        for (src, tgt), edge in self.memory.edges.items():
   304→            if edge.staleness > 0.8 and edge.retrieval_util < 0.2:
   305→                src_node = self.memory.nodes.get(src)
   306→                if src_node and src_node.stratum == Stratum.SPECULATIVE:
   307→                    report.demotions += 1
   308→                    event = ReconciliationEvent(
   309→                        event_type=ReconciliationType.DEMOTION,
   310→                        decision=ReconciliationDecision.ARCHIVE,
   311→                        node_ids=[src],
   312→                        reason=(
   313→                            f"Speculative node '{src}' has high staleness "
   314→                            f"({edge.staleness:.2f}) and low utility ({edge.retrieval_util:.2f})"
   315→                        ),
   316→                        details={
   317→                            "staleness": edge.staleness,
   318→                            "retrieval_util": edge.retrieval_util,
   319→                        },
   320→                        auto_resolved=True,
   321→                    )
   322→                    report.events.append(event)
   323→

1→"""
     2→Claim Graph + Evidence Linking — Structured claim tracking with contradiction detection.
     3→
     4→Every answer's claims are tracked as nodes in a directed graph.
     5→Evidence edges link claims to their support/contradiction relationships.
     6→Uncertainty propagates through contradictions.
     7→
     8→Architecture: ADCCL Phase 5 — Claim Accountability
     9→"""
    10→
    11→import time
    12→import uuid
    13→from dataclasses import dataclass, field
    14→from enum import Enum
    15→from typing import TYPE_CHECKING
    16→
    17→if TYPE_CHECKING:
    18→    from omega.answer import AnswerObject
    19→    from omega.retrieval import RetrievedChunk
    20→
    21→
    22→class ClaimType(str, Enum):
    23→    SUPPORTED = "supported"
    24→    INFERRED = "inferred"
    25→    CONTRADICTED = "contradicted"
    26→    UNRESOLVED = "unresolved"
    27→
    28→
    29→class EdgeRelation(str, Enum):
    30→    SUPPORTS = "supports"
    31→    CONTRADICTS = "contradicts"
    32→    QUALIFIES = "qualifies"
    33→    SUPERSEDES = "supersedes"
    34→
    35→
    36→@dataclass
    37→class ClaimNode:
    38→    claim_id: str
    39→    text: str
    40→    claim_type: ClaimType
    41→    support_strength: float  # 0-1
    42→    grounding_strength: float  # 0-1
    43→    source_refs: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
    44→    created_at: float = field(default_factory=time.time)
    45→
    46→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    47→        return {
    48→            "claim_id": self.claim_id,
    49→            "text": self.text,
    50→            "claim_type": self.claim_type.value,
    51→            "support_strength": round(self.support_strength, 4),
    52→            "grounding_strength": round(self.grounding_strength, 4),
    53→            "source_refs": self.source_refs,
    54→            "created_at": self.created_at,
    55→        }
    56→
    57→
    58→@dataclass
    59→class EvidenceEdge:
    60→    edge_id: str
    61→    source_claim_id: str
    62→    target_claim_id: str
    63→    relation: EdgeRelation
    64→    weight: float  # 0-1
    65→    source_ref: str = ""
    66→    excerpt: str = ""
    67→
    68→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
    69→        return {
    70→            "edge_id": self.edge_id,
    71→            "source_claim_id": self.source_claim_id,
    72→            "target_claim_id": self.target_claim_id,
    73→            "relation": self.relation.value,
    74→            "weight": round(self.weight, 4),
    75→            "source_ref": self.source_ref,
    76→            "excerpt": self.excerpt[:200],
    77→        }
    78→
    79→
    80→class ClaimGraph:
    81→    """
    82→    Directed graph of claims with evidence edges.
    83→    Tracks support, contradiction, qualification, and supersession.
    84→    """
    85→
    86→    def __init__(self):
    87→        self.claims: dict[str, ClaimNode] = {}
    88→        self.edges: list[EvidenceEdge] = []
    89→
    90→    def add_claim(self, claim: ClaimNode) -> ClaimNode:
    91→        self.claims[claim.claim_id] = claim
    92→        return claim
    93→
    94→    def add_edge(self, edge: EvidenceEdge) -> EvidenceEdge:
    95→        self.edges.append(edge)
    96→        return edge
    97→
    98→    def get_contradictions(self) -> list[tuple[ClaimNode, ClaimNode, EvidenceEdge]]:
    99→        """Return all contradiction triples (source_claim, target_claim, edge)."""
   100→        result = []
   101→        for edge in self.edges:
   102→            if edge.relation == EdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS:
   103→                src = self.claims.get(edge.source_claim_id)
   104→                tgt = self.claims.get(edge.target_claim_id)
   105→                if src and tgt:
   106→                    result.append((src, tgt, edge))
   107→        return result
   108→
   109→    def get_unresolved(self) -> list[ClaimNode]:
   110→        """Return all claims marked UNRESOLVED."""
   111→        return [c for c in self.claims.values() if c.claim_type == ClaimType.UNRESOLVED]
   112→
   113→    def grounding_ratio(self) -> float:
   114→        """Fraction of claims that are grounded (SUPPORTED with grounding_strength > 0.3)."""
   115→        if not self.claims:
   116→            return 0.0
   117→        grounded = sum(
   118→            1 for c in self.claims.values()
   119→            if c.claim_type == ClaimType.SUPPORTED and c.grounding_strength > 0.3
   120→        )
   121→        return grounded / len(self.claims)
   122→
   123→    def propagate_uncertainty(self) -> None:
   124→        """
   125→        Reduce support_strength of claims that are contradicted.
   126→        For each contradiction edge, the target claim's support_strength
   127→        is reduced proportionally to the edge weight and the source claim's
   128→        support_strength.
   129→        """
   130→        for edge in self.edges:
   131→            if edge.relation == EdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS:
   132→                target = self.claims.get(edge.target_claim_id)
   133→                source = self.claims.get(edge.source_claim_id)
   134→                if target and source:
   135→                    penalty = edge.weight * source.support_strength * 0.5
   136→                    target.support_strength = max(0.0, target.support_strength - penalty)
   137→                    if target.support_strength < 0.2:
   138→                        target.claim_type = ClaimType.CONTRADICTED
   139→
   140→    def to_dict(self) -> dict:
   141→        return {
   142→            "claims": {cid: c.to_dict() for cid, c in self.claims.items()},
   143→            "edges": [e.to_dict() for e in self.edges],
   144→            "grounding_ratio": round(self.grounding_ratio(), 4),
   145→            "total_claims": len(self.claims),
   146→            "total_edges": len(self.edges),
   147→            "contradictions": len(self.get_contradictions()),
   148→            "unresolved": len(self.get_unresolved()),
   149→        }
   150→
   151→    @classmethod
   152→    def from_retrieval_and_answer(
   153→        cls,
   154→        answer: "AnswerObject",
   155→        chunks: list["RetrievedChunk"],
   156→    ) -> "ClaimGraph":
   157→        """
   158→        Build a ClaimGraph from an AnswerObject and its source chunks.
   159→
   160→        Strategy:
   161→        - Split answer text into sentences as candidate claims.
   162→        - Each chunk becomes a source evidence node.
   163→        - Match claims to chunks by keyword overlap to create SUPPORTS edges.
   164→        - Claims with no chunk support are marked UNRESOLVED.
   165→        - Claims with strong chunk support are SUPPORTED.
   166→        - Claims with weak support are INFERRED.
   167→        """
   168→        graph = cls()
   169→
   170→        # Build chunk-level evidence claims
   171→        chunk_claim_ids: dict[str, str] = {}
   172→        for chunk in chunks:
   173→            cid = str(uuid.uuid4())
   174→            node = ClaimNode(
   175→                claim_id=cid,
   176→                text=chunk.content[:300],
   177→                claim_type=ClaimType.SUPPORTED,
   178→                support_strength=min(1.0, chunk.score),
   179→                grounding_strength=min(1.0, chunk.score),
   180→                source_refs=[chunk.source_ref],
   181→            )
   182→            graph.add_claim(node)
   183→            chunk_claim_ids[chunk.chunk_id] = cid
   184→
   185→        # Split answer into sentence-level claims
   186→        sentences = _split_sentences(answer.text)
   187→        answer_claim_ids: list[str] = []
   188→
   189→        for sentence in sentences:
   190→            if len(sentence.strip()) < 10:
   191→                continue
   192→            acid = str(uuid.uuid4())
   193→            # Score support by keyword overlap with chunks
   194→            best_score = 0.0
   195→            best_chunk_id = None
   196→            sentence_words = set(sentence.lower().split())
   197→
   198→            for chunk in chunks:
   199→                chunk_words = set(chunk.content.lower().split())
   200→                if not chunk_words:
   201→                    continue
   202→                overlap = len(sentence_words & chunk_words) / max(len(sentence_words), 1)
   203→                if overlap > best_score:
   204→                    best_score = overlap
   205→                    best_chunk_id = chunk.chunk_id
   206→
   207→            # Classify claim
   208→            if best_score >= 0.3:
   209→                claim_type = ClaimType.SUPPORTED
   210→                support_strength = min(1.0, best_score * 1.5)
   211→                grounding_strength = min(1.0, best_score * 1.2)
   212→            elif best_score >= 0.1:
   213→                claim_type = ClaimType.INFERRED
   214→                support_strength = best_score
   215→                grounding_strength = best_score * 0.5
   216→            else:
   217→                claim_type = ClaimType.UNRESOLVED
   218→                support_strength = 0.0
   219→                grounding_strength = 0.0
   220→
   221→            source_refs = []
   222→            if best_chunk_id:
   223→                for chunk in chunks:
   224→                    if chunk.chunk_id == best_chunk_id:
   225→                        source_refs = [chunk.source_ref]
   226→                        break
   227→
   228→            node = ClaimNode(
   229→                claim_id=acid,
   230→                text=sentence.strip(),
   231→                claim_type=claim_type,
   232→                support_strength=support_strength,
   233→                grounding_strength=grounding_strength,
   234→                source_refs=source_refs,
   235→            )
   236→            graph.add_claim(node)
   237→            answer_claim_ids.append(acid)
   238→
   239→            # Create evidence edge from chunk to answer claim
   240→            if best_chunk_id and best_chunk_id in chunk_claim_ids:
   241→                edge = EvidenceEdge(
   242→                    edge_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
   243→                    source_claim_id=chunk_claim_ids[best_chunk_id],
   244→                    target_claim_id=acid,
   245→                    relation=EdgeRelation.SUPPORTS,
   246→                    weight=best_score,
   247→                    source_ref=source_refs[0] if source_refs else "",
   248→                    excerpt=sentence[:200],
   249→                )
   250→                graph.add_edge(edge)
   251→
   252→        # Detect contradictions: answer claims that reference conflicting chunks
   253→        # Two chunks contradict if they both support the same answer claim
   254→        # but have very different content (low mutual overlap)
   255→        for i, chunk_a in enumerate(chunks):
   256→            for chunk_b in chunks[i + 1:]:
   257→                words_a = set(chunk_a.content.lower().split())
   258→                words_b = set(chunk_b.content.lower().split())
   259→                if not words_a or not words_b:
   260→                    continue
   261→                mutual = len(words_a & words_b) / max(len(words_a | words_b), 1)
   262→                # If chunks are very different but both scored, flag contradiction
   263→                if mutual < 0.1 and chunk_a.score > 0.3 and chunk_b.score > 0.3:
   264→                    cid_a = chunk_claim_ids.get(chunk_a.chunk_id)
   265→                    cid_b = chunk_claim_ids.get(chunk_b.chunk_id)
   266→                    if cid_a and cid_b:
   267→                        edge = EvidenceEdge(
   268→                            edge_id=str(uuid.uuid4()),
   269→                            source_claim_id=cid_a,
   270→                            target_claim_id=cid_b,
   271→                            relation=EdgeRelation.CONTRADICTS,
   272→                            weight=1.0 - mutual,
   273→                            source_ref=chunk_a.source_ref,
   274→                            excerpt=f"Low overlap ({mutual:.2f}) between high-scoring chunks",
   275→                        )
   276→                        graph.add_edge(edge)
   277→
   278→        return graph
   279→
   280→
   281→def _split_sentences(text: str) -> list[str]:
   282→    """Split text into sentences on period, question mark, exclamation, or newline."""
   283→    import re
   284→    # Split on sentence-ending punctuation followed by space or end
   285→    parts = re.split(r'(?<=[.!?])\s+|\n+', text)
   286→    return [p.strip() for p in parts if p.strip()]
